title: Chile: Allende to Pinochet Update — Apr 19, 2026
summary: Chile faces a major accountability crisis in early 2026. The Senate narrowly passed (23-22) a commutation bill in March that would free approximately 370 Pinochet-era human rights convicts from Punta Peuco prison — many convicted torturers and murderers. New President José Antonio Kast, who has praised Pinochet and whose cabinet includes former Pinochet defense lawyers, was inaugurated on March 11 amid warnings from human rights organizations that the Museum of Memory and Villa Grimaldi memorial face defunding. Counterbalancing forces: Australia's Federal Court cleared extradition of former DINA agent Adriana Rivas on April 7, and a CIPER Chile investigation identified a former Macul landfill that may contain remains of up to 30 disappeared persons. Meanwhile, formal criminal charges against Víctor Jara's alleged killer Pedro Pablo Barrientos moved the long-running case toward an oral criminal trial in Chile.

title: Chile: Allende to Pinochet Update — Tue Apr 7, 2026
summary: Australia Clears Extradition of Former DINA Agent Adriana Rivas After 7-Year Legal Battle.

title: Chile: Allende to Pinochet Update — Fri Mar 20, 2026
summary: CIPER Investigation: Remains of Up to 30 Disappeared Persons May Be Buried at Former Santiago Landfill.

title: Chile: Allende to Pinochet Update — Wed Mar 11, 2026
summary: José Antonio Kast, 60, was inaugurated as Chile's President on March 11, 2026 — the 36th anniversary of the restoration of democracy and the handover from Pinochet to Aylwin.

title: Chile: Allende to Pinochet Update — Wed Mar 4, 2026
summary: Chile's Senate approved 23-22 a bill allowing prisoners aged 75 or older, or those with serious illness, to serve sentences outside prison. Chile's Chamber of Deputies Votes to Make National Search Plan for Disappeared Persons a Permanent Policy.
